://>://>://>://>Hi, I'm not sure if I understand your question correctly, but I'll try to answer it as best I can. The planning phase in Kanban is done in a different way than in Scrum. In Kanban, the planning is done in a continuous manner, rather than in a timeboxed sprint. This means that work is pulled from the backlog as capacity allows, rather than being committed to a specific sprint. The team is responsible for prioritizing the work and deciding what to work on next, and the work is pulled from the backlog as capacity allows. This allows for more flexibility and adaptability, as the team can respond to changing priorities and customer needs. In Scrum, on the other hand, the planning is done in a timeboxed sprint, where the team commits to a specific set of work for a fixed period of time (usually 2-4 weeks). The team works on the committed work during the sprint, and at the end of the sprint, the team reviews the work and decides what to work on in the next sprint. This allows for a more structured and predictable approach, but may not be as flexible as Kanban. In terms of retrospectives and refinement meetings, both Kanban and Scrum have them. In Kanban, the team may hold retrospectives to reflect on the work and identify areas for improvement, and may also hold refinement meetings to prioritize and plan the work. In Scrum, the team holds retrospectives at the end of each sprint to reflect on the work and identify areas for improvement, and may also hold refinement meetings to plan the work for the next sprint. So, while the planning phase is different in Kanban and Scrum, both frameworks have similar practices for retrospectives and refinement meetings. I hope this helps! you want to filter out only those that match a specific domain, you can use a regular expression to do so. A regular expression is a pattern that describes a set of strings, and it can be used to match and extract specific parts of a string.

In this case, we want to match email addresses that end with @johnpaulprep.org. We can do this by using the following regular expression:

/^.*@johnpaulprep\.org$/

This regular expression matches any string that starts with any number of characters, followed by an @ symbol, followed by the domain name johnpaulprep.org, followed by any number of characters. The $ symbol at the end of the expression indicates that the match must end at the end of the string.

To use this regular expression in Python, you can use the re module. Here's an example of how you can use it to filter email addresses:

import re

email_addresses = ['john@example.com', 'jane@johnpaulprep.org', 'jim@johndoe.com']

filtered_email_addresses = []

for email_address in email_addresses:
if re.match(r'^.*@johnpaulprep\.org$', email_address):
filtered_email_addresses.append(email_address)

print(filtered_email_addresses)

In this example, we first import the re module. Then, we create a list of email addresses. We then use a for loop to iterate over each email address in the list, and use the re.match() method to check if the email address matches the regular expression. If it does, we add it to a new list called filtered_email_addresses. Finally, we print the filtered_email_addresses list to see the result.

In this case, the output will be:

['jane@johnpaulprep.org']

This means that the email address jane@johnpaulprep.org is the only one that matches the regular expression, and it is the only one that is returned in the filtered_email_addresses list.

Dear [Branding Expert and Marketing Expert],

We are looking for your expertise in developing our brand communications pillars and marketing strategies for our fitness app, Transform. Our goal is to empower women to achieve their health and fitness goals through personalized nutrition and expert guidance. We believe that your experience in branding and marketing can help us communicate this message effectively to our target audience.

Our brand messaging revolves around the three pillars of Connect, Empower, and Give. Connect represents support for our users and for others. Empower stands for empowering oneself and others through science and diagnostics-based approach to achieve optimal health. Give represents our commitment to measuring success in mood, sleep, energy, and physical health, and donating a portion of our proceeds to charity.

We are looking to develop a brand guideline and framework that will help us communicate a strong and consistent message to our target audience. This guideline should include our brand purpose, promise, values, personality, and tone.

We are also looking for your expertise in developing our marketing strategies, including digital and social media marketing, influencer marketing, and advertising. Our target audience is women who are pregnant or post-pregnancy, looking to transition to optimal health through personalized nutrition plans and expert guidance.

We would like to request a proposal from you outlining your approach to developing our brand guideline and marketing strategies. This proposal should include your proposed timeline, budget, and deliverables.

We look forward to hearing from you and working with you to transform the lives of women through our fitness app.

Sincerely,
[Your Name]

from bokeh.plotting import figure, output_file, show
from bokeh.models import ColumnDataSource, HoverTool
from bokeh.embed import components

# Create a figure
p = figure(x_axis_type="datetime", width=800, height=250)
p.line(x="date", y="close", source=source, line_width=2)

# Add a hover tool
hover = HoverTool(tooltips=[("Date", "@date{%F}"), ("Close", "@close")])
p.add_tools(hover)

# Create a ColumnDataSource
source = ColumnDataSource(data=dict(date=dates, close=prices))

# Add a title and axis labels
p.title.text = "Stock Prices"
p.xaxis.axis_label = "Date"
p.yaxis.axis_label = "Close Price"

# Output the plot to a file
output_file("stock_prices.html")

# Show the plot
show(p)
```
This code creates a figure with a line plot of the stock prices, and adds a hover tool to display the date and close price on hover. It also creates a `ColumnDataSource` object to store the data, and uses the `output_file` and `show` functions to output the plot to a file and display it in a browser.:// The following code is the Flask app code:
```
from flask import Flask, render_template, request, redirect, url_for
from bokeh.embed import server_document
from bokeh.plotting import figure, output_file, show
from bokeh.models import ColumnDataSource, HoverTool
from bokeh.server.server import Server
from bokeh.application import Application
from bokeh.application.handlers import FunctionHandler

# Create a Flask app
app = Flask(__name__)

# Create a Bokeh server
server = Server({"/": Application(FunctionHandler(lambda: index))})

# Create a function to render the index page
@app.route("/")
def index():
    # Get the stock prices
    prices = get_stock_prices()

    # Create a figure
    p = figure(x_axis_type="datetime", width=800, height=250)
    p.line(x="date", y="close", source=source, line_width=2)

    # Add a hover tool
    hover = HoverTool(tooltips=[("Date", "@date{%F}"), ("Close", "@close")])
    p.add_tools(hover)

    # Create a ColumnDataSource
    source = ColumnDataSource(data=dict(date=dates, close=prices))

    # Add a title and axis labels
    p.title.text = "Stock Prices"
    p.xaxis.axis_label = "Date"
    p.yaxis.axis_label = "Close Price"

    # Render the plot
    script, div = components(p)

    # Render the template
    return render_template("index.html", script=script, div=div)

# Create a function to get the stock prices
def get_stock_prices():
    # Get the stock prices from a CSV file

        for row in reader:
            prices.append(float(row[1]))
    return prices

30fps or 60fps?
When capturing video from a VHS tape using OBS, the frame rate you select will depend on the specific requirements of your project and the type of video you are working with.

The frame rate is the number of frames per second that the video is captured at. The higher the frame rate, the smoother the video will appear, but it will also require more storage space and may result in a larger file size.

If you are working with a VHS tape that has a lower resolution, such as 720p or 480p, then it's recommended to capture the video at a lower frame rate, such as 30fps. This will help to reduce the file size and make it easier to work with the video in post-production.

If you are working with a VHS tape that has a higher resolution, such as 1080p, then it's recommended to capture the video at a higher frame rate, such as 60fps. This will help to capture the video at a higher quality and make it easier to work with the video in post-production.

In summary, when capturing video from a VHS tape using OBS, the frame rate you select will depend on the specific requirements of your project and the type of video you are working with. If you are working with a lower resolution VHS tape, then it's recommended to capture the video at a lower frame rate, such as 30fps. If you are working with a higher resolution VHS tape, then it's recommended to capture the video at a higher frame rate, such as 60fps.

Use a responsive design approach: A responsive design approach involves using a combination of techniques to ensure that your app looks good and functions well on different screen sizes and orientations. This includes using flexible layouts, media queries, and responsive images.2. Use a responsive layout: A responsive layout is a layout that adjusts its size and position based on the screen size and orientation. This can be achieved using a combination of techniques such as flexbox, grid, and media queries.3. Use a responsive image: A responsive image is an image that adjusts its size and resolution based on the screen size and orientation. Use a responsive bottom sheet: A responsive bottom sheet is a bottom sheet that adjusts its size and style based on the screen size and orientation. This can be achieved using the `BottomSheet` widget and the `size` and `color` properties.13. Use a responsive snackbar: A responsive snackbar is a snackbar that adjusts its size and style based on the screen size and orientation. Yes, that's correct. A stash is typically a personal storage area for a developer, while a repository is a shared storage area for a team or organization.

A stash is a temporary storage area in a version control system, where changes to a project can be stored temporarily before they are committed to the main codebase. Stashes are typically used when a developer needs to switch to working on a different branch or task, but doesn't want to commit their changes yet. Stashes are usually local to the developer's machine, and are not shared with other members of the development team.

On the other hand, a repository is a central location where the complete history of a project is stored, along with all the files, code, and other assets that make up the project. A repository is usually shared among all members of a development team, who can access, modify, and collaborate on the project files. A repository typically includes version control tools that allow developers to track changes to the project over time, and to collaborate on the same codebase.

In summary, a stash is a temporary storage area for changes that haven't been committed to the main codebase, while a repository is a central location for the entire project, including all its

I have trapped your planet in a force field, and I have done so for a very good reason. Your planet is a cesspool of corruption, greed, and violence. You have allowed your leaders to abuse their power and exploit the weak. You have allowed your corporations to pollute the environment and destroy the natural world. You have allowed your citizens to turn a blind eye to the suffering of others.

But I have come to set things right. I have come to bring justice to your planet. I have come to show you the error of your ways.

I have trapped your planet in a force field because I want to give you a chance to change. I want to give you a chance to become a better planet. I want to give you a chance to become a planet that values compassion, empathy, and justice.

But I will not give you this chance for free. You must earn it. You must work to make your planet a better place. You must work to end corruption, greed, and violence. You must work to protect the environment and the natural world. You must work to help the weak and the oppressed.

If you do these things, I will release your planet from the force field. But if you do not, I will keep your planet trapped in the force field forever.

Mediawiki uses a combination of the `User` table and the `User_groups` table to store user information, including passwords. The `User` table stores basic user information such as the username, email address, and password hash. The `User_groups` table stores the user's group memberships, which can be used to control access to different parts of the wiki.

The password hash is stored in the `User` table using the `user_password` field. The password hash is generated using the `password_hash()` function in PHP, which uses the `PASSWORD_BCRYPT` algorithm by default. This algorithm is a one-way hash function that is designed to be slow and memory-intensive, making it difficult for attackers to crack the password.

To set up a new Mediawiki installation, you will need to create a new database and import the Mediawiki database schema. You can then use the `Special:UserLogin` page to create new users and set their passwords. Mediawiki will automatically generate a password hash for each user and store it in the `User` table. You will not need to set up your own SQL database for storing password hashes, as Mediawiki will handle this for you.

so that the output is a new excel file with a tab for each unique combination of the "CPT" and "BlueHost" columns?
Yes, you can do that by using the `groupby()` function and the `size()` function of the dataframe. Here's an example:
```
import pandas as pd

#Read in the csv file
df = pd.read_csv('your_file.csv')

#Create a new dataframe containing each unique combination of the "CPT" column and the "BlueHost" column
df_grouped = df.groupby(['CPT','BlueHost']).size().reset_index(name='counts')

#This new dataframe will have three columns, "CPT","BlueHost" and "counts"

#Output the filtered dataframe to a new excel file with a specified tab name
df_grouped.to_excel('filtered_data.xlsx', sheet_name='your_tab_name', index=False)
```
You will have to replace 'your\_file.csv' with the name of your csv file and 'your\_tab\_name' with the tab name you want the sheet to have in the excel file. The Arduino bootloader is a small program that is stored in the flash memory of an Arduino board, and is responsible for loading the main program (the sketch) from the computer and running it. When the board is powered on, the bootloader is automatically loaded and runs, and then waits for the computer to send the main program.

During the bootloader stage, the board is detected as an Arduino device, and the computer can communicate with it using the Arduino IDE or other software that supports the Arduino platform. However, the board is not detected as a keyboard device during this stage, as the bootloader does not have the necessary drivers or firmware to emulate a keyboard.

Sure thing! Here's a shopping list for the ingredients needed to make two cakes:

Ingredients:

* 2 cups all-purpose flour
* 2 teaspoons baking powder
* 1/2 teaspoon salt
* 1 cup unsalted butter, softened
* 1 1/2 cups granulated sugar
* 4 large eggs, at room temperature
* 1 teaspoon vanilla extract
* 1 cup milk, at room temperature
* 1/2 cup sour cream, at room temperature
* 1/2 cup cocoa powder
* 1/2 cup hot water
* 1/2 cup chocolate chips
* 1/2 cup chopped nuts (optional)
* 1/2 cup powdered sugar
* 1/4 cup heavy cream
* 1/4 cup chocolate syrup

Instructions:

1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C). Grease and flour two 9-inch round cake pans.
2. In a large bowl, whisk together the flour, baking powder, and salt. Set aside.
3. In another large bowl, beat the butter and sugar until light and fluffy, about 3 minutes. Add the eggs one at a time, beating well after each addition. Beat in the vanilla extract.
4. Alternately add the dry ingredients and milk mixture to the butter mixture, beginning and ending with the dry ingredients. Mix until just combined.
5. Divide the batter evenly between the prepared pans. Bake for 25-30 minutes or until a toothpick inserted into the center

and case studies. Explain how to create a UI design system and how to use it in the design process. Explain how to create a UI design system and how to use it in the design process. A UI design system is a set of design guidelines, components, and patterns that are used to create a consistent and cohesive user interface across different digital products. It helps to ensure that the design is consistent, scalable, and easy to maintain.

To create a UI design system, you need to follow these steps:

1. Define the design principles and guidelines: Start by defining the design principles and guidelines that will guide the design process. These principles should be based on the brand identity, user needs, and business goals.

2. Create a design library: Create a design library that includes all the design elements such as colors, typography, icons, buttons, forms, and other UI components. This library should be organized and easily accessible to the design team.

3. Create design patterns: Create design patterns that can be used across different digital products. These patterns should be based on the design principles and guidelines and should be reusable and scalable.

4. Create a style guide: Create a style guide that includes all the design elements, patterns, and guidelines. This guide should be used as a reference for the design team and should be updated regularly.

5. Use the design system in the design process: Use the design system in the design process to ensure consistency and cohesion across different digital products. This includes using the design library, patterns, and style guide to create the user interface.

To use the design system in the design process, follow these steps:

1. Start with the design principles and guidelines: Use the design principles and guidelines to guide the design process. This will ensure that the design is consistent and aligned with the brand identity and user needs.

2. Use the design library: Use the design library to create the user interface. This includes using the colors, typography, icons, buttons, forms, and other UI components.

3. Use the design patterns: Use the design patterns to create the user interface. This includes using the patterns that have been created for different types of interactions and user flows.

4. Use the style guide: Use the style guide to ensure consistency and cohesion across different digital products. This includes using the design elements, patterns, and guidelines to create the user interface.

5. Update the design system regularly: Update the design system regularly to ensure that it is up-to-date and aligned with the latest design trends and technologies.

By following these steps, you can create a UI design system that is consistent, scalable, and easy to maintain. This will help to ensure that the design is aligned with the brand identity, user needs, and business goals, and that it is easy to use and understand for the users.

The business owner came to me, desperation written all over their face. "We've invested millions of dollars into this project, but it's sinking fast. We're on the verge of collapse. Can you help us turn things around?"
